LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BAY AREA WAREHOUSE STORES'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE COURT FINDS NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT THAT THE CONDUCT OF RACQUEL O'BRIEN WAS REASONABLY FORESEEABLE. THUS, AS A MATTER OF LAW, DEFENDANT DID NOT OWE A DUTY TO PROTECT AGAINST AN UNFORESEEABLE CRIMINAL ACTION OF MS. O'BRIEN. (ANN M. V. PACIFIC PLAZA SHOPPING CENTER (1993) 6 CAL.4TH 666.) THE COURT ALSO DECLINES TO FASHION A DUTY UNLESS IT IS CLEARLY SUPPORTED BY PUBLIC POLICY. (LICHTMAN V. SIEMENS INDUSTRY INC. (2017) 16 CAL.APP.4TH 914, 921.) HERE, NO SUCH PUBLIC POLICY EXISTS. THE COURT ALSO FINDS NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT THAT ANY ALLEGED NEGLIGENT ACT OR OMISSION OF DEFENDANT WAS A PROXIMATE CAUSE OF PLAINTIFF'S INJURY. PLAINTIFF PRESENTED NO EVIDENCE OF A CAUSAL LINK BETWEEN PLAINTIFF'S INJURY AND ANY ALLEGED NEGLIGENT ACT OR OMISSION OF DEFENDANT. (SAELZLER V. ADVANCED GROUP 400 (2001) 25 CAL.4TH 763.) ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ANDREW Y.S. CHENG, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: REYES HUNTER, CSR #6576, RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summ Adjudication
Off Calendar Dec-27-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michelle Gildea, Esq. 415-677-0730 of Borton Petrini for Defendant BAY AREA WAREHOUSE STORES DBA FOODSCO.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BAY AREA WAREHOUSE STORES DBA FOODSCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND/Or Summry Adjudication.

Defendant Bay Area Warehouse Stores's motion for summary judgment is granted. The Court finds no triable issue of material fact that the conduct of Racquel O'Brien was reasonably foreseeable. Thus, as a matter of law, defendant did not owe a duty to protect against an unforeseeable criminal action of Ms. O'Brien. (Ann M. v. Pacific Plaza Shopping Center (1993) 6 Cal.4th 666.) The Court also declines to fashion a duty unless it is clearly supported by public policy. (Lichtman v. Siemens Industry Inc. (2017) 16 Cal.App.4th 914, 921.) Here, no such public policy exists.

The Court also finds no triable issue of material fact that any alleged negligent act or omission of defendant was a proximate cause of plaintiff's injury. Plaintiff presented no evidence of a causal link between plaintiff's injury and any alleged negligent act or omission of defendant. (Saelzler v. Advanced Group 400 (2001) 25 Cal.4th 763.)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, rhunter@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/AYSC)
